tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58601,"title":"[DevOps Infrastructure] Docs: Add code examples and enrich README for stable 1.0.0 release","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` **Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` **Triggered by:** commit 601109e01 (PR #58580) by `@azure-sdk` ## What Changed The package version was incremented to `1.1.0-beta.1` following the first stable `1.0.0` release of `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` (Managed DevOps Pools management library). The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is empty of actual code** — the README only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) with no inline examples showing how to create, list, get, or delete a pool - **Sample test files are not wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README - **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` - **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit 1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text: ```markdown To create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package: ``` dotnet add package Azure.Identity ``` Then instantiate an `ArmClient`: ``` TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); ``` ``` 2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### DevOpsPoolResource Represents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization. ### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile Describes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management. ### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile Associates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects. ### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile Configures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs). Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` 3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ### Get a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group ``` ``` ### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders. #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs` In the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with: ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool // ... (pool creation code) ... #endregion ``` Add a new minimal `Get_PoolsGet` method annotated with `[Test]` and `[Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")]`: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task Get_PoolsGet() { // get your azure access token TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool string poolName = "pool"; DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName); Console.WriteLine($"Pool name: {result.Data.Name}"); #endregion } ``` Add a new `List_PoolsList` method: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task List_PoolsList() { TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Pool: {pool.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs` Find the delete method and wrap the relevant lines: ```csharp #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool ArmOperation lro = await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); #endregion ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs` Add a `#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient` / `#endregion` block around the `ArmClient` instantiation lines. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` already contains all required sections, but the following need content added or improved: | Section | Status | Action needed | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Link only | Add code snippet placeholder | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add service-specific type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders for create/get/list/delete | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — injects `#region Snippet:` code from test files into README.md placeholders. Verify the git diff shows README updated with code. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — only needed if public types or members changed (not required for docs-only changes) 5. `dotnet format sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` — ensures consistent code formatting ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24843135704/agentic_workflow) · ● 879K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58601","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T15:28:17Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T15:28:17Z"}

The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is empty of actual code** — the README only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) with no inline examples showing how to create, list, get, or delete a pool - **Sample test files are not wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README - **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` - **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit 1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text: ```markdown To create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package: ``` dotnet add package Azure.Identity ``` Then instantiate an `ArmClient`: ``` TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); ``` ``` 2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### DevOpsPoolResource Represents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization. ### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile Describes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management. ### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile Associates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects. ### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile Configures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs). Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted) ``` 3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ### Get a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group ``` ``` ### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool ``` ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders. #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs` In the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with: ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool // ... (pool creation code) ... #endregion ``` Add a new minimal `Get_PoolsGet` method annotated with `[Test]` and `[Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")]`: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task Get_PoolsGet() { // get your azure access token TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool string poolName = "pool"; DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName); Console.WriteLine($"Pool name: {result.Data.Name}"); #endregion } ``` Add a new `List_PoolsList` method: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task List_PoolsList() { TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Pool: {pool.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs` Find the delete method and wrap the relevant lines: ```csharp #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool ArmOperation lro = await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); #endregion ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs` Add a `#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient` / `#endregion` block around the `ArmClient` instantiation lines. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` already contains all required sections, but the following need content added or improved: | Section | Status | Action needed | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Link only | Add code snippet placeholder | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add service-specific type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders for create/get/list/delete | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` README following the `1.0.0` stable release, requesting addition of code examples, enriched Key concepts, and wired snippet tags for the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tooling.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` (version bumped to `1.1.0-beta.1` post-stable)
**Affected API:** README documentation and sample test files
**Scenarios:**
- Examples section is empty — no inline code for create/get/list/delete pool operations
- Sample test files (`Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, etc.) lack `#region Snippet:` tags needed for `Update-Snippets.ps1` injection
- Key concepts section only links to generic Azure SDK docs rather than explaining service-specific types (`DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, etc.)
- Authenticate the client section links to a quickstart rather than showing an inline `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` snippet

**Root ask:** Enrich the README with code examples, service-specific key concepts, and wire existing sample files with snippet region tags.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue opened automatically by a workflow after the `1.0.0` stable release. The implementation guide in the issue body is detailed and actionable.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review the existing sample files under `tests/Samples/` and confirm they compile correctly
2. Add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` markers to sample methods per the issue's implementation guide
3. Update `README.md` to add inline snippet placeholders, auth code example, and service-specific key concepts
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` to inject code into README
5. Verify README diff looks correct and commit all changes together
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` is a management-plane SDK (Azure.ResourceManager.* prefix)
- **Service:** `DevOps` — Matches the `%DevOps` ServiceLabel entry in CODEOWNERS; service directory is `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/`
- **Confidence:** High — Package name explicitly named in issue body; category and service are unambiguous
</details>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 887) — matches because all labels in the entry ("Mgmt") are present in the predicted labels; this is the first match scanning bottom-to-top
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978

- **Routing action:** Assigned `@ArthurMa1978`
- **Scan notes:** Entries for `%Datadog %Mgmt`, `%Device Registry %Mgmt`, `%Durable Task Scheduler %Mgmt`, and other multi-label Mgmt entries (lines ~962–1159) were scanned first but all require a different service label not present in this issue. The `%DevOps` entry (line 688) has only ServiceOwners, but was not reached because the `%Mgmt` catch-all at line 887 matched first.
